description: Critical fixes from Sahara Founders WhatsApp group feedback (Feb 28 - Mar 3, 2026). Must be resolved before Palo Alto demo to 250 founders next Tuesday. Linear issues: AI-1280 through AI-1286.

userStories.title: Add forgot password / reset password flow to login page
userStories.description: Login page shows 'Incorrect password' error but has no 'Forgot password?' link. Fred Cary himself got locked out. Add Supabase Auth password reset flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page has visible 'Forgot password?' link below password field
Clicking link shows email input and sends Supabase password reset email
Password reset page exists at /reset-password or /auth/reset-password
User can set new password and login successfully
Typecheck passes
No regressions to existing login flow
userStories.notes: Linear: AI-1281. Files: app/login/, lib/auth/. Use supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail() and supabase.auth.updateUser(). Need a callback URL configured in Supabase dashboard.

userStories.title: Fix Fred AI broken/vague response state
userStories.description: Fred AI is giving vague non-answers like 'I want to give you a solid answer, but I need a few more details first.' Fred Cary reported 'Fred isn't working.' Investigate and fix root cause.
acceptanceCriteria: Fred responds with substantive, helpful answers to direct questions
Asking 'help me structure what I want to build' gets a structured response, not a deflection
Fred's system prompt includes enough context to be helpful without excessive clarifying questions
API route returns streaming response without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Linear: AI-1284. Files: lib/ai/, lib/agents/, app/api/fred/chat/route.ts. Check system prompt, model config, API keys. Fred should be proactive and structured, not endlessly asking clarifying questions.

userStories.title: Fred collects business fundamentals before deep-diving
userStories.description: When a user mentions a specific issue (e.g., unit economics), Fred dives straight into exhausting Q&A without first collecting business basics: name, sector, positioning, revenue, team size, funding stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Fred's system prompt or conversation flow collects business fundamentals first
Fundamentals include: business name, sector/industry, positioning, revenue, team size, funding stage
Only after fundamentals are gathered does Fred dive into specific issues
Gathered info is stored and used to populate founder profile
Responses are structured and not overwhelming
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Linear: AI-1282. Files: lib/agents/, lib/ai/, lib/fred/. Update Fred's system prompt to have a structured information-gathering phase before free-form advisory.

userStories.title: Fix voice chat cutting off on Samsung devices
userStories.description: Voice chat with Fred cuts off after user gets a couple words in on Samsung devices. Works on Apple and computers. LiveKit WebRTC Samsung compatibility issue.
acceptanceCriteria: Voice connection maintains for at least 60 seconds on Chrome Android
WebRTC configuration includes Samsung-compatible codec fallbacks
ICE candidate handling works across Samsung browsers
No immediate disconnect after voice connection established
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Linear: AI-1280. Files: workers/, app/chat/, components that use LiveKit. Check opus codec support, ICE config, TURN server setup. Samsung Internet and Chrome on Samsung both need testing.

userStories.title: Structured goal sets by funding stage appear on dashboard
userStories.description: After onboarding conversation with Fred, a custom goal set should appear on the dashboard based on the founder's stage: beginner (idea→fundable), pre-seed (analyze→deck→Boardy), seed (metrics→market).
acceptanceCriteria: Fred identifies founder's stage during conversation
Goal set generated based on stage and stored in database
Dashboard shows personalized goal set with milestones
Each milestone has clear actionable next steps
Beginners: idea validation → MVP → customers → fundable
Pre-seed: readiness analysis → deck review → new deck → Boardy intro
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Linear: AI-1283. Files: lib/agents/, lib/ai/, app/dashboard/. Fred should generate a scoped plan per founder that appears on homepage after chatting.

userStories.title: Fred AI health monitoring and graceful error handling
userStories.description: Add health check for Fred AI so broken states are detected early. Show user-friendly error messages instead of blank/broken states.
acceptanceCriteria: Health check endpoint at /api/fred/health returns AI availability status
If Fred AI is down, chat UI shows friendly error message with retry button
AI call timeout protection (abort after 60 seconds)
Error states logged with enough context to debug
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Linear: AI-1284 (related). Files: app/api/fred/, components/chat/. Combine with US-029 from previous audit (timeout protection).

userStories.title: Platform load readiness for 250 concurrent users
userStories.description: Verify the platform can handle 50+ simultaneous users for the Palo Alto demo. Optimize critical paths: signup → chat → dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Signup flow completes in under 5 seconds
Chat with Fred returns first streaming token in under 3 seconds
Dashboard loads all widgets in under 8 seconds
No rate limiting triggered during normal demo usage patterns
Mobile experience is polished (responsive, no overflow)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Linear: AI-1286. Cross-cutting validation. Check Vercel serverless concurrency limits, Supabase connection pooling, AI provider rate limits.
